commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=f06088d3918242ff4aa645db44931c09031adcae)
commands.review_summary:
The PR surfaces `sessions_spawn` subagent start/completion events as transient progress draft items and changes Telegram native draft sends to coalesce, abort, and stay off the final-answer path.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a feature PR, not a current-main bug report. The source path is reviewable from the patch and current tests, but no failing current-main reproduction is required.
commands.review_followup: Needs contributor or maintainer-provided real Telegram behavior proof and a maintainer decision or fix for the fallback regression; automation cannot replace the missing real-environment proof.

target.title: [codex] Surface subagent progress drafts
target.body:
## Summary

- surfaces accepted `sessions_spawn` work as keyed transient sub-agent progress items
- converts subagent completion internal events into transient progress updates
- redacts and caps subagent labels/status summaries before they can reach channel progress drafts
- maps `subagent` progress items to the existing `sessions_spawn` display entry so Telegram native draft previews can render them
- smooths Telegram native tool-progress drafts by coalescing rapid updates, blocking empty `Working`-only drafts, aborting in-flight native draft requests during teardown, and keeping slow native draft network sends off the final-answer delivery path

## Current draft status

- Draft PR intentionally stays draft.
- Follow-up to merged Telegram native draft lane from #83622.
- Rebased on current `origin/main` (`df8505b09d`).
- Local live install was updated from this branch for final user proof capture.
- Pending maintainer proof: real Telegram DM/native draft video or equivalent visible behavior proof showing no duplicate progress bubble, no draft-field flicker after final delivery, and no final-answer delay from slow native draft sends.

## Local system integration

- Built runtime dist from this branch and copied it into `/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist` after backup.
- Latest backup: `/Users/alexanderkrimm/.openclaw/backups/openclaw-dist-nonblocking-drafts-20260519-075205`
- Restarted local OpenClaw LaunchAgent gateway via `openclaw daemon restart`.
- Verified installed dist contains the non-blocking native draft `sendNow = (text) => { ... }` path and no old `return await sendNow(...)` final-path blocker.
- Verified gateway after restart: health `ok`, eventLoop `degraded=false`, Telegram connected, WhatsApp healthy, read probe ok.

## Validation

- `git diff --check`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/native-tool-progress-draft.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/channel-streaming.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.tools.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.subscribe-embedded-pi-session.subscribeembeddedpisession.test.ts`
- Result: 7 test files / 264 tests passed
- `CI=true node scripts/tsdown-build.mjs && node scripts/runtime-postbuild.mjs && node scripts/build-stamp.mjs && node scripts/runtime-postbuild-stamp.mjs`
- `openclaw gateway status --deep --require-rpc`
- `openclaw gateway health --json`

## Notes

- No final answer text is moved into native drafts; final delivery still uses the normal durable Telegram send path.
- The native draft code now passes grammY's supported `AbortSignal` argument and returns from `update()` immediately after scheduling a native draft send, so Telegram draft latency cannot hold the final message path.
- Startup logs still contain unrelated local warnings (`control-ui` assets missing, `tools.fs.workspaceOnly=false`) and one transient cron isolated-agent timeout; health is green after startup settles.
